steps.action: The Armenian dance (Armenian: Հայկական պար) heri with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The Armenian dance (Armenian: Հայկական պար) heri with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: , in the higher regions of Armenia, the land of
steps.text: , in the higher regions of Armenia, the land of Ararat, there are rock paintings of scenes of country dancing.

steps.action: These dances were most likely accompanied by cer
steps.text: These dances were most likely accompanied by certain kinds of songs or musical instruments.

steps.action: Traditional dancing is still popular among expat
steps.text: Traditional dancing is still popular among expatriate Armenians, and has also been very successfully exported to international folk dance groups and circle dance groups all over the world.

steps.action: All dancers wear the traditional costume to embo
steps.text: All dancers wear the traditional costume to embody the history of their culture and to tell their ancestors stories.

steps.action: The traditional coloring and exquisite beading o
steps.text: The traditional coloring and exquisite beading of the costumes tie the dance and the tradition together.

steps.action: The beautiful movements of the Armenian cultural
steps.text: The beautiful movements of the Armenian cultural dance are adored by audiences around the world.

steps.action: It is of interest to note that dance never occur
steps.text: It is of interest to note that dance never occurred alone, but was always accompanied with song, clapping of hands, and musical instruments.

steps.action: As with music, so too the dance expressed a pers
steps.text: As with music, so too the dance expressed a person's internal spiritual emotions and personal disposition.

steps.action: They taught ballet and character dance.
steps.text: They taught ballet and character dance.

steps.action: Shalakho (Շալախօ) - A dance for men from Ancient
steps.text: Shalakho (Շալախօ) - A dance for men from Ancient Armenia.

steps.action: It is performed in dance studios by men, but dan
steps.text: It is performed in dance studios by men, but danced at public gatherings by women as well.

steps.action: Tamzara (Թամզարա) - A dance originating in the A
steps.text: Tamzara (Թամզարա) - A dance originating in the Armenian Highlands.

steps.action: Yarkhushta (Յարխուշտա) - Yarkhushta is a martial
steps.text: Yarkhushta (Յարխուշտա) - Yarkhushta is a martial dance from the Taron and Aghdznik regions of Western Armenia.

steps.action: Berd (Բերդապար) - Berd is a dance famous for hav
steps.text: Berd (Բերդապար) - Berd is a dance famous for having a circle of men stand on the shoulders of another circle and rotate.

steps.action: Berd means "fortress" in Armenian and is named a
steps.text: Berd means "fortress" in Armenian and is named accordingly because of the shape the dancers make.

steps.action: Shirkhani (Շիրխանի) Harsnapar (Հարսնապար) - from
steps.text: Shirkhani (Շիրխանի) Harsnapar (Հարսնապար) - from the Armenian hars which means bride, and par which translates to dance.

steps.action: The bride is shown dancing a solo and may possib
steps.text: The bride is shown dancing a solo and may possibly feature the bridesmaids.

steps.action: Normally, if a man is the one dancing, the music
steps.text: Normally, if a man is the one dancing, the music will be fast-paced and in the case of a woman, a slower, more elegant music will play.

steps.action: It features a liveliness to it and normally incl
steps.text: It features a liveliness to it and normally includes both dancers doing symmetrical movements.

steps.action: Ishkhanats par (Իշխանաց պար) - Also known as the
steps.text: Ishkhanats par (Իշխանաց պար) - Also known as the "Lords' dance".

steps.action: Kesabian (Քեսապական) - The Kesabian dance came f
steps.text: Kesabian (Քեսապական) - The Kesabian dance came from the city of Kesab, Syria.

steps.action: Moosh (or Muş, or Mus, Մշո Պար) - a mixed dance
steps.text: Moosh (or Muş, or Mus, Մշո Պար) - a mixed dance from the district of Moush/Taron, west of Lake Van.

steps.action: Sasnapar (Սասնապար) - a popular dance common at
steps.text: Sasnapar (Սասնապար) - a popular dance common at weddings and other cultural gatherings.
